title: Ranch Homes for Rent in Cleveland, Ohio
metaTitle: Ranch Homes for Rent in Cleveland, Ohio
metaDescription: Looking for ranch homes for rent in Cleveland, Ohio? See typical rents by bedroom count and Section 8-friendly rental homes. Book a free showing today.
primaryKeyword: ranch homes for rent cleveland ohio
answerBox: A ranch home is a single-story house, often with an attached garage and bedrooms along one hallway rather than stacked upstairs. Rent Finder Cleveland works with rental homes across East and Southeast Cleveland, including single-family houses among our duplexes and triplexes — every home welcomes Housing Choice Vouchers. Ask about single-story layouts when you book a showing.

sections.h2: What makes a house a "ranch" rental?
body: A ranch-style home is built on a single level, usually with a low-pitched roofline, an attached or detached garage, and bedrooms laid out along one hallway instead of stacked over a first floor. The style became common across the United States starting in the 1950s, and Cleveland-area subdivisions built in that era still have a share of one-story houses mixed in with the region's older two-story housing stock.

Our own selection reflects Cleveland's real housing mix rather than a single architectural style: **we work with a combination of single-family houses, duplexes, triplexes, and fourplexes**, concentrated on the city's East and Southeast sides. Single-story layouts do turn up among the single-family homes in that mix, but we don't track "ranch" as a fixed listing category — the fastest way to find one is to [book a showing](/p/book-showing) and tell our leasing team you're looking for a single-level home.

sections.h2: Where in Cleveland do single-story rental homes show up?
body: Single-story rental homes are most likely to appear alongside our broader inventory in East and Southeast Cleveland ZIP codes — including 44105 (Slavic Village), 44110 (Collinwood), 44108 (Glenville), 44104 (Fairfax/Central), 44103 (Hough), and 44120 (Buckeye-Shaker) — with a smaller number of homes on the West side in 44102 (Cudell/Detroit-Shoreway) and 44109 (Old Brooklyn).

Because Cleveland's housing stock spans early-1900s two- and three-story homes as well as postwar single-level construction, availability of any one layout shifts as listings turn over. [See what's currently available](/cleveland-rentals/houses/houses-for-rent-cleveland-available-now/) or [browse our full list of houses for rent in Cleveland](/houses-for-rent-cleveland-oh/) to check today's single-story options.

If a specific layout matters more to you than a specific neighborhood, it's worth staying flexible on ZIP code: a single-story home might come open in Glenville one month and Buckeye-Shaker the next, simply because that's where a single-family property happens to turn over.

sections.h2: What does a ranch rental cost in Cleveland?
body: Rent for a single-story home depends mainly on bedroom count and condition rather than the roofline, so it tracks the same ranges as the rest of our available homes. Two-bedroom homes in our current Cleveland listings typically run from about $750 to $1,100 a month, three-bedroom homes from roughly $1,100 to $1,500, and larger four-bedroom homes from about $1,200 to $1,800.

For citywide context, Zumper's rent report dated July 4, 2026 put Cleveland's overall median asking rent at $1,250 a month, with two-bedroom units averaging around $1,100 and three-bedroom units near $1,350 — in the same range as our own listings. RentCafe's July 2, 2026 report put the average rent for professionally managed Cleveland apartments notably higher, at $1,564 a month, which mostly reflects larger complexes rather than the individual houses and small multi-family buildings we work with.

Often includes a basement or yard

body: Yes — every home Rent Finder Cleveland works with, single-story or not, accepts Housing Choice Vouchers (Section 8) and is kept HUD-inspection-ready. Housing quality inspections for voucher units are shifting nationwide from the older HQS standard to the newer NSPIRE standard, with [HUD's Housing Choice Voucher program](https://www.hud.gov/helping-americans/housing-choice-vouchers) setting a final transition deadline of February 1, 2027.

If you hold a voucher through the Cuyahoga Metropolitan Housing Authority (CMHA) and want a single-level layout, mention it when you [book a free showing](/p/book-showing), or read our [Cleveland Section 8 guide](/section-8-housing-cleveland-oh/) for how the local voucher process works.

faq.q: Are there ranch-style houses for rent in Cleveland?
faq.a: Some single-story homes are mixed into our Cleveland selection alongside duplexes, triplexes, and two-story single-family houses concentrated on the East and Southeast sides. Availability changes as listings turn over, so the fastest way to find a single-level layout is to book a showing and tell our leasing team what you're looking for.

faq.q: Do ranch homes cost more to rent than two-story houses?
faq.a: Not typically. Rent in our Cleveland listings is driven mainly by bedroom count and condition, not roofline. Two-bedroom homes generally run $750–$1,100 a month and three-bedroom homes $1,100–$1,500, whether the layout is one level or two.

faq.q: Do single-story rentals accept Section 8 vouchers?
faq.a: Yes. Every home we work with, regardless of style, accepts Housing Choice Vouchers and is HUD-inspection-ready. Voucher holders should still confirm rent and payment-standard details with CMHA before applying.

faq.q: What Cleveland neighborhoods have the most rental homes available?
faq.a: Our Cleveland inventory is concentrated in Slavic Village (44105), Collinwood (44110), Glenville (44108), Fairfax/Central (44104), Hough (44103), and Buckeye-Shaker (44120), with a smaller number of homes on the West side in Cudell/Detroit-Shoreway (44102) and Old Brooklyn (44109).

faq.q: How do I ask about a specific layout before touring?
faq.a: Book a free showing through our site and tell the leasing team what you need — single-story, a certain bedroom count, or a specific budget. We'll point you toward current listings that match as closely as possible.
